# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Service configuration that exposes our application to the internet using a LoadBalancer. The app serves HTTP traffic on port 80 and HTTPS on port 443. Make it accessible from any source for maximum flexibility. This is for our public-facing API.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1   # Kubernetes version, usually "v1" or a higher number depending upon the feature you are using 
kind: Service    # The resource kind to create (e.g., Pods) which is this case `Service` for exposing our app on external network in terms of load balancing and networking  
metadata:       
  name: my-app     # Name given给资源, used as identifier throughout the kubernetes cluster   
spec:              # Specifications about how to create resources like Pods or other services/configmaps (like 'type', depending on what we are creating), etc. Here you can specify more details  
  type: LoadBalancer     # Type of K8S resource that this service creates, in our case "Load balancer" because it'll distribute network traffic to multiple Pods using cloud provider load-balancing feature (usually a third party). Also supports Ingress if defined. This means the resources are exposed on an external IP address or within kubernetes cluster and we can use this service for end users by passing their application public DNS name/IP in place of actual pod's ip
  selector:      # K8S label-selector which defines how to select Pod(s) that will get traffic from the LoadBalancer. When a change happens, only those replicas selected via our 'app=my-app', are reloaded or scaled up/down  
    app: my-app  # Our custom selector - it means K8S should use this label to select and forward流量 into Pods named with name `'selector`'. The pod is matched based on these labels. In case if our 'my_label=value', then the match will be successful only for those particular application running under process that has my-app:tag set
  ports:            # Define how Kubernetes service should expose this Pod to external traffic, e.g., mapping port from within pod or outside world (like HTTP and HTTPS)  
    - protocol: TCP          # Protocol used for communication between our app container inside the cluster & other services/pods on network — usually 'tcp' but can also be UDP depending upon use case, defaults to tcp if not specified. If we specify something else here it will take precedence and is considered as primary protocol
      port: 80               # Main ports exposed by service outside world (like HTTP traffic) on which our Pods are running in Kubernetes cluster  
    - protocol: TCP          # Similar explanation for second main external accessibility option we're exposing to end users, defaults or can be UDP also if not specified. It is considered as a backup and fallback from 'primary'. This part should always exist unless you want this service open externally but no app client found on the public network
      port: 443              # Second main external accessibility option we're exposing to end users, also running in Kubernetes cluster
